headline: Apollo Global Management (APO) Is Up 5.5% After Backing Broadcom’s $35 Billion AI Infrastructure Push
summary: Recently, Broadcom announced it is launching the AI XPV Platform with Apollo Global Management and Blackstone, where Apollo is leading a US$35.00 billion capital solution intended to support more than 20 gigawatts of AI compute capacity globally through 2028, including funding over 1 gigawatt of additional infrastructure for Anthropic. This move highlights how Apollo is positioning itself as a key capital provider for large-scale AI infrastructure, potentially reinforcing its role in...

headline: Apollo & Blackstone Inc. (BX) Seal $35B Anthropic AI Deal
summary: Blackstone Inc. (NYSE:BX) is a deep value stock to invest in now. On June 6, 2026, Apollo Global Management (NYSE:APO) and Blackstone (NYSE:BX) finalized a $35 billion financing package for Anthropic, according to Bloomberg. The deal is one of the largest private credit transactions on record and will fund Google’s custom TPUs, leased by Anthropic […]

headline: Apollo seeks Japan insurance licence amid regulatory hurdles – report
summary: The US asset manager held discussions to acquire two subsidiaries of T&D Holdings as well as a life insurance unit belonging to Japanese investment firm Orix, with the latter talks described as being at an early stage.

content.title: Apollo seeks Japan insurance licence amid regulatory hurdles – report
content.summary: The US asset manager held discussions to acquire two subsidiaries of T&D Holdings as well as a life insurance unit belonging to Japanese investment firm Orix, with the latter talks described as being at an early stage.

thumbnail.caption: Apollo's interest in Japan comes against a backdrop of slowing demand for retirement products in the US and growing regulatory pushback in Europe. Credit: Piotr Swat/Shutterstock.com.

headline: Warren Wants Wall Street to Disclose Data on AI Firm Exposure
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Senator Elizabeth Warren introduced a bill requiring financial institutions to disclose their exposure to artificial intelligence companies, continuing her push for more scrutiny on the sector’s funding.
